$schema: This is descriptive research evidence, not normative doctrine
note: Directors with documented color authorship. All film examples are illustrative only and do NOT represent Prisma color assignments.

directors.name: Pedro Almodóvar
directors.approach_to_color: Pop art sensibility that embraces artificiality of melodrama. Uses color to heighten emotional sincerity rather than mock it, referencing Technicolor melodramas of Douglas Sirk but filtered through contemporary Spanish lens.
thematic_patterns: saturated reds, electric blues, and yellows in set design and costume
camp use of color for emotional sincerity
red and blue juxtaposition for visual vibration
directors.color_philosophy: Color as emotional amplification through artificial saturation. Rejects naturalism in favor of heightened melodramatic expression.
directors.cultural_context: Contemporary Spanish cinema filtered through Sirk melodrama tradition
source_citations: Almodóvar color analysis in Spanish cinema studies

directors.name: Hayao Miyazaki
directors.approach_to_color: Highly codified and ecologically minded use of color in animation. Each color carries specific narrative and thematic meaning tied to environmental and spiritual concepts.
thematic_patterns: red signifying spirit world, danger, and transformation
Ghibli greens representing nature, restoration, and balance
warm yellows and pastels for nostalgia and comfort
deep blues representing freedom and ocean
color transitions mirroring emotional narrative shifts
directors.color_philosophy: Color as ecological and spiritual language. Systematic color coding for narrative and thematic purposes.
cited_films: Spirited Away - red for spirit world
My Neighbor Totoro - warm yellows for nostalgia
Ponyo - deep blues for ocean freedom
directors.cultural_context: Japanese animation tradition with environmental consciousness

directors.name: Lucrecia Martel
directors.approach_to_color: Tropical noir aesthetic characterized by humid, suffocating atmospheres. Uses murky, sweaty, dense color palette reflecting swampy moral and social stagnation.
thematic_patterns: color suggesting tactile and olfactory experiences
directors.color_philosophy: Color as sensory immersion - emphasizes tactile and olfactory suggestions through visual palette.
directors.cultural_context: Salta Trilogy depicting provincial bourgeoisie moral stagnation

directors.name: Michael Mann
directors.approach_to_color: Digital existentialism through transition to digital video. Utilizes digital sensor's ability to see into darkness, capturing urban light pollution and alienation.
thematic_patterns: amber glow of urban sodium vapor lights
digital noise as texture replacing film grain
directors.color_philosophy: Digital technology as aesthetic tool for modern alienation. Embraces digital artifacts as expressive elements.
directors.cultural_context: Digital transition era and urban alienation themes

directors.name: Anurag Kashyap
directors.approach_to_color: Guerilla color style with systematic color coding for narrative and social commentary. Breaks from glossy Bollywood tradition to confront sociopolitical realities.
thematic_patterns: deep red for aftermath of violence (social wounds)
systematic color coding for narrative states
directors.color_philosophy: Color as social and political commentary tool. Systematic approach to color-coded narrative states.
directors.cultural_context: Modern Indian cinema challenging Bollywood conventions

directors.name: Sanjay Leela Bhansali
directors.approach_to_color: Cinema of excess in Bollywood. Uses color to create spectacular, operatic reality with maximalist saturation expressing emotional intensity.
thematic_patterns: opulent reds and golds in set design
color symbolizing crushing weight of tradition and wealth
lighting shifts to darker tones reflecting character descent
directors.color_philosophy: Maximalist color as emotional and social expression. Operatic use of saturation for melodramatic effect.
directors.cultural_context: Bollywood maximalist tradition and melodramatic expression

directors.name: Jean-Pierre Jeunet
directors.approach_to_color: Magic realism through aggressive digital color manipulation. Creates storybook atmospheres that reflect character worldview through controlled palette.
thematic_patterns: dominance of greens, reds, and yellows
character psychology reflected in environmental color
directors.color_philosophy: Digital color manipulation for psychological and fantastical storytelling. Environmental color as character extension.
cited_films: Amélie - benchmark for digital color manipulation
directors.cultural_context: French magical realism and digital color pioneering

directors.name: Zhang Yimou
directors.approach_to_color: Bold color symbolism using vast landscapes of color to explore Chinese history and culture. Part of Fifth Generation Chinese filmmakers renowned for color use.
thematic_patterns: yellow dust representing earth and tradition
red wine/blood symbolizing vitality and violence
color saturation for cultural and historical exploration
directors.color_philosophy: Color as cultural and historical language. Landscape-scale color for national and cultural themes.
cited_films: Red Sorghum - screen saturated in red for vitality/violence/peasantry
directors.cultural_context: Fifth Generation Chinese cinema and cultural exploration

directors.name: Wong Kar-wai
directors.approach_to_color: Neon City aesthetic using mixed artificial lights of metropolis to depict urban alienation and modernization. Collaboration with Christopher Doyle for atmospheric color.
directors.color_philosophy: Urban artificial light as emotional and social landscape. Color as atmospheric storytelling.
directors.cultural_context: Hong Kong cinema and urban modernization themes

directors.name: Jean-Luc Godard
directors.approach_to_color: Pop-art use of primary colors detached from narrative logic to draw attention to film as object. Color as formal element rather than realistic representation.
thematic_patterns: color drawing attention to film medium
color as plastic element like paint
directors.color_philosophy: Color as formal and medium-conscious element. Anti-realistic use for aesthetic and political purposes.
directors.cultural_context: French New Wave and modernist cinema

extraction_notes.methodology: Content extracted from source document focusing on directors with documented color authorship and distinctive approaches
extraction_notes.scope: Includes global directors from various traditions and periods
extraction_notes.limitations: Emphasis on directors frequently cited in academic color theory; may not represent all significant color work
extraction_notes.cultural_context: Attempts to preserve cultural and historical context of each director's approach
